%-------------------------
% TORTURE TEST RESUME
% Every construct the parser claims to support, plus deliberate nastiness:
% special chars, nested braces, duplicate names, comments containing fake
% sections, empty args, math, hrefs, multi-line items, orphan lists.
%------------------------

\documentclass[letterpaper,11pt]{article}

\usepackage{latexsym}
\usepackage[empty]{fullpage}
\usepackage{titlesec}
\usepackage[usenames,dvipsnames]{color}
\usepackage{enumitem}
\usepackage[hidelinks]{hyperref}
\usepackage{fancyhdr}
\usepackage[english]{babel}
\usepackage{tabularx}
\input{glyphtounicode}

\pagestyle{fancy}
\fancyhf{}
\renewcommand{\headrulewidth}{0pt}
\renewcommand{\footrulewidth}{0pt}

\addtolength{\oddsidemargin}{-0.5in}
\addtolength{\evensidemargin}{-0.5in}
\addtolength{\textwidth}{1in}
\addtolength{\topmargin}{-.5in}
\addtolength{\textheight}{1.0in}

\urlstyle{same}
\raggedbottom
\raggedright
\setlength{\tabcolsep}{0in}

\titleformat{\section}{
  \vspace{-4pt}\scshape\raggedright\large
}{}{0em}{}[\color{black}\titlerule \vspace{-5pt}]

\pdfgentounicode=1

% Custom commands (the classic Jake set, including the S variant)
\newcommand{\resumeItem}[1]{
  \item\small{
    {#1 \vspace{-2pt}}
  }
}

\newcommand{\resumeSubheading}[4]{
  \vspace{-2pt}\item
    \begin{tabular*}{0.97\textwidth}[t]{l@{\extracolsep{\fill}}r}
      \textbf{#1} & #2 \\
      \textit{\small#3} & \textit{\small #4} \\
    \end{tabular*}\vspace{-7pt}
}

\newcommand{\resumeSubheadingS}[2]{
  \vspace{-2pt}\item
    \begin{tabular*}{0.97\textwidth}[t]{l@{\extracolsep{\fill}}r}
      \textbf{#1} & #2 \\
    \end{tabular*}\vspace{-7pt}
}

\newcommand{\resumeProjectHeading}[2]{
    \item
    \begin{tabular*}{0.97\textwidth}{l@{\extracolsep{\fill}}r}
      \small#1 & #2 \\
    \end{tabular*}\vspace{-7pt}
}

\newcommand{\resumeSubItem}[1]{\resumeItem{#1}\vspace{-4pt}}

\newcommand{\resumeSubHeadingListStart}{\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=0.15in, label={}]}
\newcommand{\resumeSubHeadingListEnd}{\end{itemize}}
\newcommand{\resumeItemListStart}{\begin{itemize}}
\newcommand{\resumeItemListEnd}{\end{itemize}\vspace{-5pt}}

%-------------------------------------------
% The following commented block must be IGNORED by the parser entirely:
% \section{Fake Commented Section}
%   \resumeSubHeadingListStart
%     \resumeSubheading{Ghost Corp}{Never}{Nowhere}{2099}
%       \resumeItemListStart
%         \resumeItem{This bullet must never appear in parsed output}
%       \resumeItemListEnd
%   \resumeSubHeadingListEnd
%-------------------------------------------

\begin{document}

\begin{center}
    \textbf{\Huge \scshape Torture T\'est\'er} \\ \vspace{1pt}
    \small 555-000-1111 $|$ \href{mailto:t@t.io}{\underline{t\_test@t.io}} $|$
    \href{https://example.com/a_b_c}{\underline{example.com/a\_b\_c}}
\end{center}

% Section with special chars in the name and duplicate company names inside
\section{R\&D Experience \& Absurdity}
  \resumeSubHeadingListStart

    \resumeSubheading
      {Research Assistant (100\% time)}{Jan. 2020 -- Present}
      {Ampersand \& Sons, Inc.}{Austin, TX}
      \resumeItemListStart
        \resumeItem{Handled budgets of \$1,200,000+ (a 45\% increase) across 3 fiscal years \& 12 departments}
        \resumeItem{Built \textbf{nested \emph{deeply \textit{formatted}}} pipelines with 99.9\% uptime}
        \resumeItem{Improved $O(n^2)$ algorithm to $O(n \log n)$, cutting latency by $\sim$40\%}
      \resumeItemListEnd

    \resumeSubheading
      {Junior Research Assistant}{June 2018 -- Dec. 2019}
      {Ampersand \& Sons, Inc.}{Austin, TX}
      \resumeItemListStart
        \resumeItem{Same employer as above: duplicate-company anchoring must attach THIS list to THIS entry}
        \resumeItem{Wrote reports using 100\_percent\_snake\_case\_file\_names.md and C\# tooling}
      \resumeItemListEnd

    \resumeSubheadingS
      {Ampersand \& Sons, Inc. $|$ Contractor}{2017}{}
      \resumeItemListStart
        \resumeItem{S-variant with a stray third brace group AND the same company name a third time}
      \resumeItemListEnd

  \resumeSubHeadingListEnd

% Empty args + trailing decoration
\section{Teaching}
  \resumeSubHeadingListStart
  \resumeSubheading
      {Course Assistant}{2023--2025}{}{}\vspace{-12pt}
      \resumeItemListStart
       \resumeItem{Entry with two empty trailing args and trailing \vspace macro}
     \resumeItemListEnd
  \resumeSubHeadingListEnd

% Projects with heavily nested braces in the title
\section{Projects}
    \resumeSubHeadingListStart
      \resumeProjectHeading
          {\textbf{\href{https://github.com/x/y_z}{\underline{Deep\{Brace\} Project}}} $|$ \emph{Python, C\#, F\#}}{2024}
          \resumeItemListStart
            \resumeItem{Title contains escaped braces \{like this\}, an href, underline, and emph all nested}
            \resumeItem{Second bullet mentions 50\%+ improvement \& \$0 budget}
          \resumeItemListEnd
      \resumeProjectHeading
          {\textbf{Tiny} $|$ \emph{One-bullet project}}{2023}
          \resumeItemListStart
            \resumeItem{Single bullet, minimal project}
          \resumeItemListEnd
    \resumeSubHeadingListEnd

% Orphan item list: bullets with no subheading at all
\section{Honors \& Awards}
\resumeSubHeadingListStart
    \resumeItemListStart
        \resumeItem{Dean's List (all semesters) \null\hfill 2020--2024}
        \resumeItem{1st place, Hackathon of 500+ teams \null\hfill 2023}
    \resumeItemListEnd
  \resumeSubHeadingListEnd

% Starred section written in plain LaTeX (no template commands)
\section*{Community Involvement}
\textbf{Torture Testers Anonymous}, Remote \hfill 2021 -- Present \\
\textit{Founding Member \& Chief Chaos Officer}
\begin{itemize}
    \item Plain-LaTeX entry under a STARRED section heading; must parse company, role, and dates.
    \item A very long bullet that wraps many lines in any editor because it keeps going and going with more detail about achievements, metrics like 12,345 users served, 67\% retention, \$89k saved, and a trailing math expression $x \geq y$ for good measure, ensuring multi-line item extraction works.
\end{itemize}

\textbf{Second Plain Org}, Somewhere \hfill 2020 \\
\textit{Member}
\begin{itemize}
    \item Second plain entry to test consecutive heading/itemize pairing.
\end{itemize}

% Skills written in the label-inside-itemize style
\section{Technical Skills}
 \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=0.15in, label={}]
    \small{\item{
     \textbf{Languages}{: Python, C\#, F\#, R, SQL (Postgres \& MySQL)} \\
     \textbf{Esoterica}{: LaTeX3, TikZ, \%-encoding, \$-signs, 100\_underscores} \\
    }}
 \end{itemize}

\end{document}
